title: Add M5 Worker projections and parity
summary: Prove Node Worker and browser Worker parity for M5 read-only projections with content, anomaly, faction, governance and event basis metadata.
acceptance: Node Worker and browser Worker consume the same M5 command stream and compare authoritative hashes at fixed checkpoints
Read-only projections include content manifest anomaly roster faction/governance season event validation and review basis metadata with canonical projection hashes
Stale projection basis is rejected or discarded with structured reasons and UI cannot repair authoritative state
Existing Worker message families are reused where feasible; any public protocol change blocks for separate review
Focused tests and e2e smoke cover projection byte size parity checkpoint diagnostics stale rejection and no UI authority

history.details: Created by WM-0072 M5 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Expose M5 content-framework state through read-only Worker projections and prove Node/browser parity.
forbiddenPaths: public Worker protocol redesign without separate gate
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: projection fields become protocol changes; use existing message families where feasible or block.
Risk: UI accepts stale content basis; tests must assert stale rejection/discard paths.
Risk: Worker parity hides runtime hash mismatch; compare both authoritative and read-model/projection hashes.
benchmarkImpact: Adds projection byte size stale rejection and Worker parity diagnostics; baseline update waits for WM-0083.
reviewRouting: Simulation-engineer completes to reviewer; client-engineer and systems-architect must be consulted before protocol/client surface changes.
rollbackModel: Revert M5 Worker projection/parity changes artifacts docs and report; leave save/replay and scenario harness intact.
ownerGate: Reuse existing protocol message families where feasible
Compare Node and browser projection hashes
Block on public protocol save dependency UI authority or M6 scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Worker parity and protocol-adjacent projection work require simulation/client architecture review.
